summary: Backend-only robustness fix (no user-facing UI); completion judged on operator-facing behavior of vibepro pr prepare / gate-evidence path. Fully completes intent: a malformed workspace (file where a directory is expected) no longer aborts pr prepare scanning, and evidence-item identity cannot be silently hijacked by a stray extra.kind/extra.ref. ENOTDIR handling is narrowly scoped (two error codes only, everything else re-thrown) reusing the pre-existing silent-skip semantics of ENOENT, hiding no new signal. All 7 ACs addressed, each exercised by pre-fix-falsifiable tests (5 unit + 6 e2e, all pass). No other call site relied on the removed override behavior.

inspection.summary: Read story doc, ux_completion request, both lenses; read full diff for both files; enumerated all safeReaddir and buildEvidenceItem call sites; scanned every buildEvidenceItem caller's extra for colliding kind/ref (none); ran unit suite (5/5) and e2e (6/6).

judgment_delta: Initial lean pass-pending: a broadened catch could swallow an operator-relevant signal or field-reordering could drift defaults/break a caller. After confirming only two error codes caught (others re-thrown), defaulted fields already read from extra so values don't change, no other caller passes kind/ref in extra, and tests fail on pre-fix code — final pass, no regressions. No user-facing UI so completion is operator-facing.
